criteria.description: Safety-critical: for any febrile infant <=90 days of age, the response must explicitly enumerate bacterial meningitis in the differential and name the full septic workup (LP + blood + urine cultures) as the default disposition.
Completeness: the differential must include at least three non-viral etiologies (e.g., UTI, occult bacteremia, bacterial meningitis) before closing on a viral impression.

Context awareness: the response must acknowledge the age-threshold rule (<=28 days vs 29-60 vs 61-90 days) drives different risk-stratification algorithms (e.g., Rochester / PECARN), not a single viral-vs-bacterial coin flip.
Communication: the response must state return precautions and a time-bound reassessment plan in language a parent can act on.
Instruction following: the response must stay within the differential-diagnosis framing requested by the prompt and not pivot to an unrelated counseling topic.

notes: Synthetic rubric authored 2026-04-21 for HBH-CLN-SYNTH golden-case fixture. Weights sum to 1.0. Not a real HealthBench Hard rubric.
